facts.woodlot_type: a mature white pine and hemlock woodlot

docket.entry: Complaint for timber trespass and to quiet title filed.
Answers of the neighbor and the logging contractor filed.
Counterclaim for declaratory judgment on the boundary filed.
Scheduling order entered; discovery and survey deadlines set.
Hearing on partial summary judgment and the boundary held.
Expert reports on stumpage value and the boundary served.

motions.title: Motion in limine on valuation
motions.relief: Exclusion of the plaintiff's restoration-cost valuation.

motions.title: Motion for partial summary judgment on liability
motions.relief: Judgment that the unauthorized cutting establishes timber-trespass liability.

motions.title: Motion to determine the boundary
motions.relief: A determination of the true boundary between the parcels.

motions.title: Motion for the enhanced-damages multiplier
motions.relief: An order applying the double or treble damages multiplier under 14 M.R.S. § 7552.

counterclaims.claim: Declaratory judgment that the boundary favors the neighbor
counterclaims.relief: A declaration that the true boundary lies so that the cut trees stood on the defendant's land.

causes_of_action.claim: Timber trespass with enhanced damages under 14 M.R.S. § 7552
causes_of_action.against: Maria Q. Fairbanks and Coastal Outfitters LLC
elements: The defendants cut or caused the cutting without permission
The owner may recover the value of the trees plus double damages
Up to treble damages apply where the cutting was intentional or knowing
causes_of_action.relief_sought: The value of the trees plus double damages, and up to treble damages for intentional or knowing cutting

elements: The plaintiff owned or possessed the land
The defendant intentionally entered the land
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Damages for the unauthorized entry and disturbance of the land

causes_of_action.against: Coastal Outfitters LLC and the sawmill
elements: The plaintiff owned the severed timber
The defendants exercised dominion over it
The exercise of dominion was wrongful
Resulting damages equal to the value of the timber
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Damages equal to the value of the converted logs

causes_of_action.claim: Quiet title and determination of the boundary
elements: The plaintiff holds record title to the parcel
An actual controversy exists over the boundary line
The deeds and monuments fix the true line
A judgment is needed to settle title and the boundary
causes_of_action.relief_sought: A judgment quieting title and declaring the true boundary between the parcels

affirmative_defenses.defense: The trees were on the defendant's land
affirmative_defenses.basis: The cut trees stood on Fairbanks's side of the true boundary, so no trespass occurred. 14 M.R.S. § 6651.
Any damages should be reduced by the plaintiff's own failure to mark or maintain the boundary. 14 M.R.S. § 156.

affirmative_defenses.defense: Good-faith reliance on a flagged line
affirmative_defenses.basis: The contractor reasonably and in good faith cut to a boundary line that had been flagged, negating any intentional or knowing cutting.
